tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4130103676,"number":57414,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Unable to use Azure.AI.Projects with service principal","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2

### Describe the bug

I have an  Agent in the new AI Foundry portal, and I have an application using `Azure.AI.Projects` to access the agent.

If I use `DefaultAzureCredential()`, it all works if I use `ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret)` it just returns empty string, with no indication of failures.

My principle has the following roles:
* Reader
* Azure AI User
* Cognitive Services User
* Cognitive Services OpenAI User

I understand that this is not likely related to the SDK (apart from not getting any error messages), but I hope someone can shine some light on this 🤞 


### Expected behavior

If the Service Principal does not have required privileges, I would expect some exception

### Actual behavior

The response comes back as an empty string
````csharp
ResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse("Tell me a joke");
var response = response.GetOutputText(); // response is empty
```

### Reproduction Steps

1. Create agent i new AI Foundry
2. Create a service principal 
3. Open IAM for the Foundry project and assign roles to the service principal  (`Reader`,`Azure AI User`, `Cognitive Services User` and `Cognitive Services OpenAI User`)
4. Create C# console app and add the following nuget packages:
* Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1
* Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2
* Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1
* Azure.Identity 1.19.0
* OpenAI 2.9.1
5. Add and run the following code:
```csharp
using Azure.AI.Projects;
using Azure.Identity;
using OpenAI.Responses;

#pragma warning disable OPENAI001

const string projectEndpoint = "...";
const string agentName = "...";
const string tenantId = "...";
const string clientId = "...";
const string clientSecret = "...";

// This works
//AIProjectClient projectClient = new(endpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint), tokenProvider: new DefaultAzureCredential());

// This fails
var credential = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);
AIProjectClient projectClient = new AIProjectClient(endpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint), tokenProvider: credential);

var responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(agentName);

Console.WriteLine("Ask me something");
var prompt = Console.ReadLine();
while (prompt != null)
{
    // Use the agent to generate a response
    ResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(prompt);

    Console.WriteLine($"OUTPUT: {response.GetOutputText()}");
    Console.WriteLine("Ask me something else");
    prompt = Console.ReadLine();
}
```

### Environment

```bash
.NET SDK:
 Version:           10.0.103
 Commit:            c2435c3e0f
 Workload version:  10.0.100-manifests.c992be6d
 MSBuild version:   18.0.11+c2435c3e0

Runtime Environment:
 OS Name:     Windows
 OS Version:  10.0.26220
 OS Platform: Windows
 RID:         win-x64
 Base Path:   C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\10.0.103\

Host:
  Version:      10.0.3
  Architecture: x64
  Commit:       c2435c3e0f

.NET SDKs installed:
  3.1.426 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  5.0.408 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  6.0.203 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  6.0.428 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  7.0.120 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  8.0.100-rc.2.23502.2 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
  10.0.103 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
```","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"wmmihaa","id":3214367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMyMTQzNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3214367?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/wmmihaa","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmmihaa/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-24T19:12:40Z","updated_at":"2026-03-24T19:12:40Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57414/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM72LGF8"},{"id":3360695636,"number":52283,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Best practice for chat completion operations with the latest projects SDK","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects 1.2.0-beta.1

### Query/Question

I've migrated our codebase to the latest foundry-projects based SDK per the guides and documentation here: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/Azure.AI.Projects_1.0.0-beta.10/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/AGENTS_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md

We have a middleware wrapper that effectively abstracts the underlying calls so we can invoke agents or chat completion requests using the same pattern.

That said, I'm a bit confused on which of the Chat Completion APIs/SDKs I am supposed to use at this point?

Do I use the AIProjectClient's `GetAzureOpenAIChatClient`() call to get a ChatCompletionClient (singular) ?

Do I use the AIProjectClient's `GetChatCompletionsClient`() call to get a ChatCompletion**s**Client (plural)?

### Environment

arguments.body: Thanks for the feedback! We are routing this to the appropriate team for follow-up. cc @dargilco @nick863 @trangevi

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** The issue reports that using `ClientSecretCredential` (service principal) with `Azure.AI.Projects` 2.0.0-beta.2 returns an empty response from `GetOutputText()`, while `DefaultAzureCredential` works correctly. The author suspects the issue may be a missing role assignment on the service principal, but is also noting the SDK provides no error/exception to help diagnose the problem.

<details>
<summary>🔍 Analysis Details</summary>

### Issue Classification
- **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects` 2.0.0-beta.2
- **Category:** `Client` — this is a data-plane SDK (non-management)
- **Service:** `AI Projects` — maps to `/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects`
- **Label confidence:** High — the package is explicitly named, the category (Client) and service (AI Projects) are unambiguous

### Possible Root Causes

1. **Missing RBAC role for service principal:** The assigned roles (`Reader`, `Azure AI User`, `Cognitive Services User`, `Cognitive Services OpenAI User`) may be insufficient for the AI Foundry Responses API with a service principal. Some operations may require additional roles such as **Azure AI Developer** or **Cognitive Services OpenAI Contributor**.

2. **Silent failure / missing error propagation in the SDK:** The `GetOutputText()` method returning an empty string instead of throwing an exception when authentication fails or the request is silently rejected is a legitimate SDK behavior concern. There should ideally be an error surface for this scenario.

3. **Credential scope mismatch:** `ClientSecretCredential` and `DefaultAzureCredential` may request different token scopes or audiences. The service may be silently returning an empty/filtered response for tokens that lack the correct scope.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Suggested Debugging Steps</summary>

1. **Verify the credential is acquiring a token** by wrapping it:
   ```csharp
   var credential = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);
   var token = await credential.GetTokenAsync(new TokenRequestContext(new[] { "https://ai.azure.com/.default" }));
   Console.WriteLine("Token acquired: " + token.Token[..20] + "...");
   ```

2. **Check if the `ResponseResult` has additional properties** beyond `GetOutputText()` — look for `Status`, error fields, or inspect the raw response if available.

3. **Try adding the `Azure AI Developer` role** to your service principal, as `Azure AI User` alone may not grant permission for Responses API calls.

4. **Enable HTTP logging** to inspect the raw requests/responses:
   ```csharp
   using Azure.Core.Diagnostics;
   using var listener = AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger(EventLevel.Verbose);
   ```

5. **Compare token claims** between the `DefaultAzureCredential` token and the `ClientSecretCredential` token using https://jwt.ms to identify scope or role differences.

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Related Resources</summary>

- [Azure AI Projects SDK README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/README.md)
- [Azure AI Foundry RBAC documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/rbac-ai-foundry)
- [Azure.Identity ClientSecretCredential docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.identity.clientsecretcredential)
- [Azure SDK logging guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/logging)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Similar Issues</summary>

No exact duplicates found for this specific `ClientSecretCredential` + `Azure.AI.Projects` behavior. Issue [#52283](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/52283) is a related question about `Azure.AI.Projects` chat completion APIs but is a distinct concern.

</details>
